questions.question: Given a dataset with values 2, 4, 6, 8, 10, find the 25th and 75th percentiles and calculate the interquartile range (IQR).
questions.hint: First arrange the data in ascending order, then find the positions of the 25th and 75th percentiles in the dataset.

questions.question: For the following dataset: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, find the 25th percentile and describe the implications of this value in the context of the data.
questions.hint: First arrange the data in ascending order, then find the position of the 25th percentile in the dataset. Consider how this value might affect the analysis of the data.

questions.question: A student's scores in a mathematics test are: 80, 70, 90, 85, 75. Calculate the 25th and 75th percentiles and the interquartile range (IQR) for these scores.
questions.hint: First arrange the data in ascending order, then find the positions of the 25th and 75th percentiles in the dataset. Calculate the IQR as the difference between the 75th percentile (Q3) and the 25th percentile (Q1).
